tasks.title: Review current product (self) and produce product analysis
tasks.description:
Perform a thorough review of the current product in the repository (project root). Actions to perform:
- Read README, docs, design notes, issues, and source code to determine current capabilities, CLI/API surface, plugin/extension points, architecture, major modules, deployment model, and existing feature list.
- Identify gaps and pain points vs. typical user needs (UX, CLI ergonomics, missing integrations, performance, security, test coverage, docs).
- Note any technical constraints or architectural decisions that would affect adding features (e.g., monolith vs plugin system, language/platform limits, dependencies).
- Produce a clear, structured markdown file named PRODUCT_ANALYSIS.md containing:
  - Executive summary (1-2 paragraphs)
  - Detailed feature list (what exists)
  - Gaps and missing features (bullet list, each with short rationale)
  - Architectural notes (key modules, data flow, extension points)
  - Risks and constraints (compatibility, licensing, security)
  - Suggested priority areas to explore further for SWARMOUT2.md
- Include paths and code pointers (file names and short code excerpts or function/class names) that implement major features so other agents can reference them.
Deliverable: PRODUCT_ANALYSIS.md in repository root.
Depends_on: []

tasks.title: Analyze competitor: claude-code-sourcemap
tasks.description:
Independently analyze the competitor located at ../claude-code-sourcemap. Actions:
- Open and read the codebase at ../claude-code-sourcemap; if README or docs exist, read them first.
- Identify and list all user-facing features, CLI commands, integrations, and unique capabilities.
- Produce an architecture summary (how major features are implemented, key modules and files, data formats used, third-party services).
- Extract code pointers: specific files/functions/classes that implement important features with short explanations and line references.
- Identify strengths and weaknesses vs. our product (functional gaps, UX advantages, performance features, missing safeguards, license implications).
- For each capability that we do not have, propose whether it should be added (yes/no/maybe) with a brief rationale and an estimated implementation complexity (small/medium/large) and estimated engineering effort in days.
- Produce CLAUDE_ANALYSIS.md containing the above sections and a short prioritized list of suggested features to consider adopting.
Deliverable: CLAUDE_ANALYSIS.md in repository root.
Depends_on: []

tasks.title: Analyze competitor: OpenCode (../opencode)
tasks.description:
Independently analyze the competitor located at ../opencode. Actions:
- Read the OpenCode source at ../opencode, including README/docs.
- Identify user-facing features, UX flows, CLI/API, integrations, and unique capabilities.
- Summarize architecture and key implementation points (modules, protocols, important files).
- Provide code pointers to files/functions implementing highlighted features with short descriptions.
- Compare strengths/weaknesses to our product and note any missing features we should consider.
- For each suggested adoption, give a short rationale and estimate complexity (small/medium/large) and days of work.
- Produce OPENCODE_ANALYSIS.md with all findings and a prioritized suggestions list.
Deliverable: OPENCODE_ANALYSIS.md in repository root.
Depends_on: []

tasks.title: Analyze competitor: copilot-cli
tasks.description:
Independently analyze copilot-cli. Actions:
- First look for a local copy (../copilot-cli). If not present, clone the public repository from GitHub (https://github.com/github/copilot-cli or the canonical repo) into a temporary workspace and read its README and source.
- Identify and document user-facing features, CLI commands, integrations (editors, terminals), auth/credential flows, and notable UX patterns.
- Summarize architecture and implementation points (how it hooks into editors/terminals, background daemons, protocol used with model backends).
- Provide file/function pointers for key features and short explanations.
- Compare with our product and propose features that would materially improve our product, with rationale and complexity/effort estimates.
- Produce COPILOT_ANALYSIS.md containing the above and a short prioritized suggestions list.
Deliverable: COPILOT_ANALYSIS.md in repository root.
Depends_on: []

tasks.title: Analyze competitor: roocode
tasks.description:
Independently analyze roocode. Actions:
- Look for a local copy (../roocode). If not found, locate the official repository on GitHub and clone it into a temporary workspace for analysis.
- Identify user-visible features, integrations, strengths, and any novel approaches or UX patterns.
- Summarize architecture, important files, and key implementation techniques.
- Provide code pointers to files/functions that enable important behavior.
- Compare to our product and list suggested features to adopt, with rationale and complexity and estimated days of work.
- Produce ROOCODE_ANALYSIS.md with findings and prioritized suggestions.
Deliverable: ROOCODE_ANALYSIS.md in repository root.
Depends_on: []

tasks.title: Create SWARMOUT2.md: consolidate analyses, recommend features, and produce implementation plan
tasks.description:
Consume the outputs from the product review and all competitor analyses (PRODUCT_ANALYSIS.md, CLAUDE_ANALYSIS.md, OPENCODE_ANALYSIS.md, COPILOT_ANALYSIS.md, ROOCODE_ANALYSIS.md) and produce the final SWARMOUT2.md document. Actions:
- Consolidate findings into a single executive summary comparing our product to competitors and highlighting gaps and opportunities.
- Create a deduplicated list of recommended feature additions, each with:
  - Short description
  - Why it matters (user/customer benefit)
  - Inspired-by (which competitor or internal gap)
  - Estimated complexity (small/medium/large) and engineering days
  - Risks and required architecture changes
  - Suggested acceptance criteria and basic test cases
- Organize the feature additions into a project plan with milestones and tasks. For each milestone include:
  - Milestone name and goal
  - Start and target duration (weeks or sprints)
  - Constituent tasks (developer tasks, QA, docs, CI/tests, security review)
  - Task dependencies (technical order)
  - Suggested owner role for each task (e.g., frontend dev, backend dev, infra, QA, docs)
  - Rough estimate for each task in days or story points
- Produce a roadmap with 3-5 milestones (e.g., Quick wins, Core features, Integrations, Performance/Hardening) and a recommended timeline (calendar weeks or sprint-count) and priorities.
- Include a recommended resource plan (headcount/roles required) to implement the roadmap within the estimated timeline.
- Add an implementation checklist with recommended CI, testing, and documentation steps to ensure quality and rollout.
- Ensure SWARMOUT2.md is actionable: every suggested feature maps to tasks and milestones with acceptance criteria and estimates.
- Place SWARMOUT2.md in repository root and include links/references to the individual analysis files created by other agents.
Dependencies: This task depends on s1, s2, s3, s4, s5 (the analysis outputs).
Deliverable: SWARMOUT2.md in repository root.
Depends_on: ["s1","s2","s3","s4","s5"]
